ticket.subject: Fw: Important security update for Australia Post APIs

ticket.description:
Hi,  
Do we have to do with this in our Api.

Thanks and regards

BHAVIK

Keeping your API connections secure
  |

  |

  |
|  
|  
| 
| 
|  
Hi Bhavik,

At Australia Post, we are committed to continually strengthening the security of our digital services.
 
 We're writing to advise that from 7 July, we will uplift our security policy for Transport Layer Security (TLS), disabling TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 across our Shipping and Tracking APIs. TLS certificates are used for API based services. 

 These changes help ensure compliance with current industry security standards. 

What this means for you
 
 After this change, only TLS 1.2 and above will be accepted, and all integrations will be required to support Server Name Indication (SNI). Clients that do not support TLS 1.2+ and SNI will no longer be able to connect. 
 
This change applies to all APIs and endpoints listed in our developer documentation:
    
   -  Postage Assessment Calculator (PAC) API: https://developers.auspost.com.au/apis/pac/reference
   - Shipping & Tracking API: https://developers.auspost.com.au/apis/shipping-and-tracking/reference
 
What you need to do
 
Please work with your IT team, developers, or integration partner to ensure:
    
   - Your API integration, middleware, or partner platform explicitly supports TLS 1.2 or higher.
   - Your integration libraries, frameworks, or network components are configured to initiate outbound connections using TLS 1.2 or higher.
   - Your integration uses a client library or framework that supports SNI.
 
 If you are unsure, we recommend explicitly configuring your client to enforce TLS 1.2 (or higher) for all outbound API calls to Australia Post. Visit our Developer Centre for more information. 
 
Need assistance?
 
If you have questions or need support validating your integration, please contact Lodgement Support or your usual Australia Post representative.
 
Thanks,

The Australia Post team
  |

  |

  |

  |

  |
|  
|  
|  
|  
|  |

  | Australia Post will never send you an unsolicited email asking for your password, credit card details or account information. |

| 
| 
|  This email was sent by Australia Post, 480 Swan Street, RICHMOND VIC 3121. You're receiving this email because you're an Australia Post customer. This is a service communication. If you have any concerns, or don't think you should have received this email, please call us on 13 POST (13 7678). For more information, visit Australia Post – Online security.
 
 Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, viruses or interference. |

comments.html_body:
Hi,  

Do we have to do with this in our Api.

Thanks and regards

BHAVIK

----- Forwarded message -----
From: "Australia Post" <noreply@email.auspost.com.au>
To: "pearl60648@yahoo.com" <pearl60648@yahoo.com>
Cc:
Sent: Tue, 7 Apr 2026 at 4:01 pm
Subject: Important security update for Australia Post APIs
Australia Post
Upcoming requirements for Shipping & Tracking integrations.
 
VIEW ONLINE
 

Keeping your API connections secure

Hi Bhavik,

At Australia Post, we are committed to continually strengthening the security of our digital services.

We're writing to advise that from 7 July, we will uplift our security policy for Transport Layer Security (TLS), disabling TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 across our Shipping and Tracking APIs. TLS certificates are used for API based services.

These changes help ensure compliance with current industry security standards.

What this means for you

After this change, only TLS 1.2 and above will be accepted, and all integrations will be required to support Server Name Indication (SNI). Clients that do not support TLS 1.2+ and SNI will no longer be able to connect.

This change applies to all APIs and endpoints listed in our developer documentation:

  • Postage Assessment Calculator (PAC) API: https://developers.auspost.com.au/apis/pac/reference
  • Shipping & Tracking API: https://developers.auspost.com.au/apis/shipping-and-tracking/reference

What you need to do

Please work with your IT team, developers, or integration partner to ensure:

  • Your API integration, middleware, or partner platform explicitly supports TLS 1.2 or higher.
  • Your integration libraries, frameworks, or network components are configured to initiate outbound connections using TLS 1.2 or higher.
  • Your integration uses a client library or framework that supports SNI.

If you are unsure, we recommend explicitly configuring your client to enforce TLS 1.2 (or higher) for all outbound API calls to Australia Post. Visit our Developer Centre for more information.

Need assistance?

If you have questions or need support validating your integration, please contact Lodgement Support or your usual Australia Post representative.

Thanks,

The Australia Post team
Australia Post will never send you an unsolicited email asking for your password, credit card details or account information.
This email was sent by Australia Post, 480 Swan Street, RICHMOND VIC 3121. You're receiving this email because you're an Australia Post customer. This is a service communication. If you have any concerns, or don't think you should have received this email, please call us on 13 POST (13 7678). For more information, visit Australia Post – Online security.

Australia Post does not represent, warrant or guarantee that the integrity of this email communication has been maintained nor that the communication is free of errors, viruses or interference.
Terms and Conditions      Help & Support      Privacy Statement     
 
© 2026 Australia Post. All Rights Reserved.
Australia Post acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the land on which we operate, live and gather as employees, and recognise their continuing connection to land, water and community. We pay respect to Elders past, present and emerging.

Thank you for sharing the email from Australia Post with us.

We will review the message internally and if any changes are needed from our end, we will take them forward.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
